title: SnowMapper: operational snow monitoring for Central Asia
description: Operational snow cover analysis at 500m resolution spanning the Tien Shan, Gissar-Alay, Pamir, and Hindukush mountain ranges, supporting water resource management across five Central Asian countries.

title: CROMO-ADAPT: cryosphere monitoring and climate adaptation
description: Co-PI and technical lead on SDC-funded programme developing operational cryosphere monitoring tools and climate assessments for national hydromet services across Central Asia.

title: Revival of Balochistan Water Resources Programme
description: Climate downscaling and hydrological modelling across 214 catchments in Balochistan, with an interactive climate and hydrology dashboard for scenario exploration.

title: UNESCO/GEF climate impact scenarios for Central Asia
description: High-resolution 21st century climate impact scenarios for snow cover across five Central Asian countries, informing national and regional climate adaptation programmes.

title: WMO Global Cryosphere Watch snow assessments
description: Contributing author producing annual snow assessments for WMO's Global Cryosphere Watch programme, covering Central Asian mountain regions.

title: Himalayan mountain hazard assessment
description: Assessment of glacier and permafrost hazards in the Indian Himalayan Region, contributing to technical guidance on mountain risk reduction for development agencies.

title: WWCS: weather and climate services for Tajikistan
description: Co-PI delivering operational weather and climate tools and training for national hydromet services in Tajikistan through a partnership approach with CARITAS Switzerland.

title: Glacio-hydrological modelling in Uttarakhand, India
description: Modelling present and future hydrological scenarios for the Bhagirathi sub-basin; co-authored glacio-hydrological modelling guidelines for High Mountain Asia.

title: World Bank: Tajikistan disaster forensics
description: Forensic analysis of cryospheric and hydrometeorological factors behind the May 2021 Tajikistan disasters using remote sensing and numerical modelling.

title: Chamoli rock-ice avalanche investigation
description: Contributed to the international investigation of the 2021 Chamoli disaster in the Indian Himalaya, published in Science.

title: Permian Global: tropical deforestation monitoring
description: Developed automated remote sensing tools for deforestation rate estimation supporting conservation finance across tropical forest regions.

title: WMO cryosphere observation network assessment
description: Inventory and gap analysis of mountain cryosphere observation networks across five Central Asian countries, delivering recommendations published as WMO GCW Report No. 1.

title: Zarafshan River Basin snow monitoring
description: Technical assistance to the Ministry of Energy and Water Resources developing pilot monitoring and modelling for snow-based water resources in the Zarafshan basin.

title: TopoPyScale: climate downscaling toolchain
description: Open-source Python library for efficient climate downscaling in complex mountain terrain, evolved from the PhD-era TopoSUB/TopoSCALE tools and now underpinning SnowMapper operations.

title: Himalaya permafrost monitoring, Nepal
description: Established high-altitude permafrost monitoring sites in the Langtang valley; trained Nepalese scientists and conducted a regional permafrost workshop.

title: DEZA climate adaptation baseline mapping, India
description: Developed baseline maps for land surface variables in the Indian Himalaya, coordinating with government agencies and the Department of Science & Technology.

title: Kunduz River Basin water resources, Afghanistan
description: Multiple engagements including water strategy development, snow sampling training, and a 30-year satellite snow cover analysis for the Kunduz River Basin.

title: Panj-Amu River Basin remote sensing, Afghanistan
description: Remote sensing analysis of irrigated zones, avalanche hazard risk zoning, and GIS capacity building for Afghan water management partners.
